reason: De Niro’s Oscar-winning turn as a tormented boxer propels this film into an acting tour-de-force.
Susan Sarandon’s Oscar-winning performance as a death row nun is a profound showcase of acting artistry.
Robert De Niro delivers a landmark, unforgettably intense performance in Scorsese’s dark character study.
F. Murray Abraham’s chilling portrayal of Salieri earned him an Oscar for one of cinema’s great lead performances.
Anthony Hopkins’s hypnotic, Oscar-winning turn as Hannibal Lecter redefined the power of a single central performance.
Orson Welles’s career-defining portrayal of Charles Foster Kane remains the gold standard for screen acting.
Marlon Brando’s Kafkaesque, Oscar-winning turn as Vito Corleone is a masterclass in cinematic performance.
Ray Liotta and De Niro bring electrifying authenticity to Scorsese’s searing, character-driven crime epic.
Samuel L. Jackson’s monologue and Travolta’s revival both underscore this film’s powerhouse, performance-driven storytelling.
Bette Davis’s razor-sharp, Oscar-nominated portrayal of aging star Margo Channing is a timeless acting showcase.
